model.owner.int_leads__enriched.name: int_leads__enriched
model.owner.int_leads__enriched.description:
One row per lead, enriched with opportunity data and derived engagement metrics. Left joins stg_gtm__leads to stg_gtm__opportunities via converted_opportunity_id — 90% of leads have no opportunity and will have null opportunity fields.
Channel is derived from form_submission_date: inbound leads submitted a form via paid advertising; outbound leads were BDR-sourced cold contacts.
Introduces two key derived metrics: speed_to_first_contact_hours (inbound only) and days_in_funnel (channel-aware start point). Both cast to their final numeric types at introduction — extract(epoch from interval) returns float8 in postgres.

lead_sk.name: lead_sk
lead_sk.description:
Surrogate primary key generated via `dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key()`. Deterministic
md5 hash of the natural key column(s). Used as the stable join key for downstream models. Natural key: lead_id.

lead_id.name: lead_id
lead_id.description: Salesforce lead identifier. Source primary key from stg_gtm__leads.

converted_opportunity_id.name: converted_opportunity_id
converted_opportunity_id.description: Foreign key to opportunities. Null for unconverted leads (90%).

opportunity_sk.name: opportunity_sk
opportunity_sk.description: Surrogate key from stg_gtm__opportunities. Null for unconverted leads.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.description: Salesforce account identifier. Null for unconverted leads.

channel.name: channel
channel.description:
Sales channel derived from lead data. Inbound leads have a non-null `form_submission_date` —
the prospect submitted an interest form via paid advertising (Facebook, Google).
Outbound leads have no form submission — they were identified and cold-contacted by
the BDR team. This is the authoritative channel signal available in the leads data.
Derived via the `derive_channel` macro in `int_leads__enriched`.

stage_name.name: stage_name
stage_name.description: Current opportunity funnel stage. Null for unconverted leads.

is_won.name: is_won
is_won.description: True if opportunity stage is Closed Won. Null for unconverted leads.

is_lost.name: is_lost
is_lost.description: True if opportunity stage is Closed Lost. Null for unconverted leads.

is_open.name: is_open
is_open.description: True if opportunity is in an active non-closed stage. Null for unconverted leads.

attribution_source.name: attribution_source
attribution_source.description: How the prospect heard about Owner. From opportunities. 76% null overall.

lost_reason.name: lost_reason
lost_reason.description: Categorized loss reason. Null for non-lost opportunities.

demo_held.name: demo_held
demo_held.description: Whether a demo was conducted. Null for unconverted leads.

demo_set_date.name: demo_set_date
demo_set_date.description: Date demo was scheduled. Null for unconverted leads.

close_date.name: close_date
close_date.description: Opportunity close date. Null for unconverted leads.

is_converted.name: is_converted
is_converted.description: True if lead status is Converted.

is_disqualified.name: is_disqualified
is_disqualified.description: True if lead is in a terminal non-converting status.

sales_call_count.name: sales_call_count
sales_call_count.description: Total calls made to this lead. 21% null.

sales_text_count.name: sales_text_count
sales_text_count.description: Total texts sent to this lead. 52% null.

sales_email_count.name: sales_email_count
sales_email_count.description: Total emails sent to this lead. 48% null.

total_activity_count.name: total_activity_count
total_activity_count.description:
Sum of all logged sales touchpoints for a lead: calls + texts + emails.
Null counts coalesced to 0 before summing. Measures engagement intensity —
higher activity counts on unconverted leads may signal friction or poor fit,
while lower counts on converted leads may signal high-quality prospect targeting.

connected_with_decision_maker.name: connected_with_decision_maker
connected_with_decision_maker.description: Whether SDR reached a decision maker.

predicted_monthly_gmv_usd.name: predicted_monthly_gmv_usd
predicted_monthly_gmv_usd.description:
Estimated monthly Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) if the restaurant adopts Owner.
Sourced from `leads.predicted_sales_with_owner`. Parsed from European decimal format
(comma separator) in staging.

Used as an LTV proxy throughout this project — actual transaction-level revenue data
is not available in the source dataset. Owner's pricing model generates revenue via
a 5% take rate on online orders, so `predicted_monthly_gmv_usd * 0.05 * 12` yields
an estimated annual revenue contribution per customer.

olo_tools_cleaned.name: olo_tools_cleaned
olo_tools_cleaned.description: Cleaned comma-separated first-party online ordering tools.

form_submission_date.name: form_submission_date
form_submission_date.description: Inbound form submission date. Null for outbound leads.

first_sales_call_at.name: first_sales_call_at
first_sales_call_at.description: Timestamp of first sales call. 23% null.

first_text_sent_at.name: first_text_sent_at
first_text_sent_at.description: Timestamp of first text sent. 60% null.

first_meeting_booked_at.name: first_meeting_booked_at
first_meeting_booked_at.description: Timestamp of first meeting booked. 89% null.

last_sales_call_at.name: last_sales_call_at
last_sales_call_at.description: Timestamp of most recent call from leads.

last_sales_activity_at.name: last_sales_activity_at
last_sales_activity_at.description: Timestamp of most recent activity across all channels. 15% null.

last_sales_email_at.name: last_sales_email_at
last_sales_email_at.description: Timestamp of most recent email. 48% null.

opportunity_created_at.name: opportunity_created_at
opportunity_created_at.description: Timestamp opportunity was created in Salesforce.

opp_last_sales_call_at.name: opp_last_sales_call_at
opp_last_sales_call_at.description: Most recent AE call timestamp from opportunities table.

speed_to_first_contact_hours.name: speed_to_first_contact_hours
speed_to_first_contact_hours.description:
Hours elapsed between a lead's inbound form submission and the first logged sales call.
Computed for inbound leads only — outbound leads have no reference start event so this
field is null for outbound. Computed via the `hours_between` macro.

A key leading indicator: research and GTM best practice consistently show that
faster response to inbound interest correlates with higher conversion rates.
Use to identify SDR response time patterns and set contact SLA targets.

days_in_funnel.name: days_in_funnel
days_in_funnel.description:
Number of days a lead has been active in the sales funnel. Channel-aware start point:

- Inbound: `form_submission_date` → `last_sales_activity_at`
- Outbound: `first_sales_call_at` → `last_sales_activity_at` (best available proxy —
  may understate true duration if BDR outreach preceded the first logged call)

Null when required timestamps are missing. Use to identify stalled leads and
benchmark expected time-to-close by channel.

model.owner.int_leads__enriched.raw_code:
-- grain: one row per lead
-- natural key: lead_id
-- upstream: stg_gtm__leads, stg_gtm__opportunities
--
-- modeling decisions:
--   - channel derived from form_submission_date: inbound leads have a form submission,
--     outbound leads are BDR-sourced cold contacts with no form. this is the authoritative
--     channel signal available in the data.
--   - speed_to_first_contact_hours: computed for inbound only (form_submission_date →
--     first_sales_call_at). outbound has no reference start point — first_sales_call_at
--     is the beginning of the engagement, not a response to an event. nulled for outbound
--     with documentation. this is a key leading indicator for inbound conversion.
--   - days_in_funnel: channel-aware start point.
--       inbound  → form_submission_date to last_sales_activity_at
--       outbound → first_sales_call_at to last_sales_activity_at (best available proxy)
--     null when required timestamps are missing.
--   - opportunity fields joined via converted_opportunity_id → opportunity_id.
--     left join preserves all leads including unconverted (90% of leads have no opportunity).
--
-- trade-offs:
--   - outbound days_in_funnel uses first_sales_call_at as a proxy start.
--     this understates true funnel duration if BDR outreach preceded the first logged call.
--   - stage_name and is_won/is_lost pulled from opportunities at current snapshot —
--     no historical stage progression available in source data.
--
-- type notes:
--   - extract(epoch from interval) returns float8 in postgres.
--     speed_to_first_contact_hours and days_in_funnel are cast to their final
--     numeric types here

{{
    config(
        materialized='view',
        contract={"enforced": true}
    )
}}

with leads as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_gtm__leads') }}
),

opportunities as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_gtm__opportunities') }}
),

joined as (
    select
        -- keys
        l.lead_sk,
        l.lead_id,
        l.converted_opportunity_id,
        o.opportunity_sk,
        o.account_id,

        -- channel derivation
        {{ derive_channel('l.form_submission_date') }}                          as channel,

        -- funnel state from opportunity (null for unconverted leads)
        o.stage_name,
        o.is_won,
        o.is_lost,
        o.is_open,
        o.attribution_source,
        o.lost_reason,
        o.demo_held,
        o.demo_set_date,
        o.close_date,

        -- lead lifecycle
        l.status,
        l.is_converted,
        l.is_disqualified,

        -- engagement activity (integer types from staging, passed through unchanged)
        l.sales_call_count,
        l.sales_text_count,
        l.sales_email_count,
        l.total_activity_count,
        l.connected_with_decision_maker,

        -- restaurant firmographics
        l.location_count,
        l.predicted_monthly_gmv_usd,
        l.marketplaces_used_cleaned,
        l.olo_tools_cleaned,
        l.cuisine_types_cleaned,

        -- timing fields passed through from staging (already typed correctly)
        l.form_submission_date,
        l.first_sales_call_at,
        l.first_text_sent_at,
        l.first_meeting_booked_at,
        l.last_sales_call_at,
        l.last_sales_activity_at,
        l.last_sales_email_at,
        o.created_at                                                            as opportunity_created_at,
        o.demo_scheduled_at,
        o.last_sales_call_at                                                    as opp_last_sales_call_at,

        -- speed to first contact: inbound only
        -- hours_between returns float8 via extract(epoch); cast to final type here
        case
            when l.form_submission_date is not null
            then (
                {{ hours_between('l.form_submission_date::timestamp', 'l.first_sales_call_at') }}
            )::numeric(10,2)
        end                                                                     as speed_to_first_contact_hours,

        -- days in funnel: channel-aware, float8 cast to final type here
        case
            when l.form_submission_date is not null
            then (
                {{ hours_between('l.form_submission_date::timestamp', 'l.last_sales_activity_at') }}
                / 24.0
            )::numeric(8,2)
            else (
                {{ hours_between('l.first_sales_call_at', 'l.last_sales_activity_at') }}
                / 24.0
            )::numeric(8,2)
        end                                                                     as days_in_funnel

    from leads l
    left join opportunities o
        on l.converted_opportunity_id = o.opportunity_id
)

select * from joined

        o.is_lost,

        o.attribution_source,
        o.lost_reason,
        o.demo_held,
        o.demo_set_date,
        o.close_date,

        -- lead lifecycle
        l.status,
        l.is_converted,
        l.is_disqualified,

        -- engagement activity (integer types from staging, passed through unchanged)
        l.sales_call_count,
        l.sales_text_count,
        l.sales_email_count,
        l.total_activity_count,
        l.connected_with_decision_maker,

        -- restaurant firmographics
        l.location_count,
        l.predicted_monthly_gmv_usd,
        l.marketplaces_used_cleaned,
        l.olo_tools_cleaned,
        l.cuisine_types_cleaned,

        -- timing fields passed through from staging (already typed correctly)
        l.form_submission_date,
        l.first_sales_call_at,
        l.first_text_sent_at,
        l.first_meeting_booked_at,
        l.last_sales_call_at,
        l.last_sales_activity_at,
        l.last_sales_email_at,
        o.created_at                                                            as opportunity_created_at,
        o.demo_scheduled_at,
        o.last_sales_call_at                                                    as opp_last_sales_call_at,

        -- speed to first contact: inbound only
        -- hours_between returns float8 via extract(epoch); cast to final type here
        case
            when l.form_submission_date is not null
            then (
                
    case
        when l.form_submission_date::timestamp is not null and l.first_sales_call_at is not null
        then extract(epoch from (l.first_sales_call_at - l.form_submission_date::timestamp)) / 3600.0
    end

            )::numeric(10,2)
        end                                                                     as speed_to_first_contact_hours,

        -- days in funnel: channel-aware, float8 cast to final type here
        case
            when l.form_submission_date is not null
            then (
                
    case
        when l.form_submission_date::timestamp is not null and l.last_sales_activity_at is not null
        then extract(epoch from (l.last_sales_activity_at - l.form_submission_date::timestamp)) / 3600.0
    end

                / 24.0
            )::numeric(8,2)
            else (
                
    case
        when l.first_sales_call_at is not null and l.last_sales_activity_at is not null
        then extract(epoch from (l.last_sales_activity_at - l.first_sales_call_at)) / 3600.0
    end

                / 24.0
            )::numeric(8,2)
        end                                                                     as days_in_funnel

    from leads l
    left join opportunities o
        on l.converted_opportunity_id = o.opportunity_id
)

model.owner.int_expenses__by_channel_mon.name: int_expenses__by_channel_month
model.owner.int_expenses__by_channel_mon.description:
One row per sales channel per calendar month. Joins advertising spend and salary/commission costs into a single unified cost table with channel attribution.
Modeling decision: advertising spend is attributed exclusively to inbound. All paid advertising (Facebook, Google) drives inbound form submissions — outbound BDR activity generates no ad spend, only headcount cost. This is not an assumption; it follows directly from the business model. Salary costs are already split by channel in the source and require no allocation.
This model is the authoritative CAC cost denominator. total_cost_usd is the correct per-channel per-month cost basis for CAC calculation in mart_cac_ltv.
Trade-offs documented:
  - Advertising cannot be split within inbound (e.g. Facebook vs Google) —
    source provides monthly aggregate only. Noted as a data gap.
  - 6 months of data (Jan–Jun 2024) limits trend analysis. Conclusions
    should be treated as directional rather than statistically definitive.

expense_channel_month_sk.name: expense_channel_month_sk
expense_channel_month_sk.description:
Surrogate primary key generated via `dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key()`. Deterministic
md5 hash of the natural key column(s). Used as the stable join key for downstream models. Natural key: month_date + channel.

month_date.name: month_date
month_date.description: First day of the calendar month. Parsed from Mon-YY source format.

channel.name: channel
channel.description:
Sales team channel. One of `inbound` (SDR-driven, paid ad traffic) or
`outbound` (BDR-driven, cold outreach). Derived from source column name during unpivot
in `stg_gtm__expenses_salary_and_commissions`.

salary_and_commissions_usd.name: salary_and_commissions_usd
salary_and_commissions_usd.description: Total salary and commission cost for this channel and month.

advertising_spend_usd.name: advertising_spend_usd
advertising_spend_usd.description: Paid advertising spend for this channel and month. Non-zero for inbound only — outbound receives 0.

total_cost_usd.name: total_cost_usd
total_cost_usd.description: Total channel cost for the month. Sum of salary and advertising. Cast to numeric(12,2) at point of introduction — addition of two numeric(12,2) values produces numeric(13,2) in postgres without cast.

model.owner.int_expenses__by_channel_mon.raw_code:
-- grain: one row per sales channel per calendar month
-- natural key: month_date + channel
-- upstream: stg_gtm__expenses_advertising, stg_gtm__expenses_salary_and_commissions
--
-- modeling decisions:
--   - advertising spend attributed exclusively to inbound. all paid advertising
--     (Facebook, Google) drives inbound form submissions — outbound BDR activity
--     generates no ad spend, only headcount cost. follows directly from the business model.
--   - salary costs already split by channel in source, require no allocation.
--   - total_cost_usd is the authoritative CAC cost denominator per channel per month.
--
-- trade-offs:
--   - advertising cannot be split within inbound (e.g. Facebook vs Google) —
--     source provides monthly aggregate only. noted as a data gap.
--   - 6 months of data (Jan–Jun 2024) limits trend analysis.
--
-- type notes:
--   - numeric(12,2) + numeric(12,2) produces numeric(13,2) in postgres.
--     total_cost_usd cast to numeric(12,2) at point of introduction.
--     advertising_spend_usd coalesced to 0 for outbound rows before summing.

{{
    config(
        materialized='view',
        contract={"enforced": true}
    )
}}

with salary as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_gtm__expenses_salary_and_commissions') }}
),

advertising as (
    select * from {{ ref('stg_gtm__expenses_advertising') }}
),

joined as (
    select
        s.month_date,
        s.sales_channel                                                 as channel,
        s.salary_and_commissions_usd,

        -- advertising attributed to inbound only; outbound receives 0
        case
            when s.sales_channel = 'inbound'
            then coalesce(a.advertising_spend_usd, 0)
            else 0::numeric(12,2)
        end                                                             as advertising_spend_usd,

        -- numeric(12,2) + numeric(12,2) = numeric(13,2) in postgres
        -- cast to numeric(12,2) here — point of introduction for this field
        (
            s.salary_and_commissions_usd
            + case
                when s.sales_channel = 'inbound'
                then coalesce(a.advertising_spend_usd, 0)
                else 0::numeric(12,2)
              end
        )::numeric(12,2)                                               as total_cost_usd

    from salary s
    left join advertising a
        on s.month_date = a.month_date
),

final as (
    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['month_date', 'channel']) }}   as expense_channel_month_sk,
        month_date,
        channel,
        salary_and_commissions_usd,
        advertising_spend_usd,
        total_cost_usd
    from joined
)

select * from final

model.owner.metricflow_time_spine.compiled_code:
with days as (
    





with rawdata as (

    

    

    with p as (
        select 0 as generated_number union all select 1
    ), unioned as (

    select

    
    p0.generated_number * power(2, 0)
     + 
    
    p1.generated_number * power(2, 1)
     + 
    
    p2.generated_number * power(2, 2)
     + 
    
    p3.generated_number * power(2, 3)
     + 
    
    p4.generated_number * power(2, 4)
     + 
    
    p5.generated_number * power(2, 5)
     + 
    
    p6.generated_number * power(2, 6)
     + 
    
    p7.generated_number * power(2, 7)
     + 
    
    p8.generated_number * power(2, 8)
     + 
    
    p9.generated_number * power(2, 9)
     + 
    
    p10.generated_number * power(2, 10)
     + 
    
    p11.generated_number * power(2, 11)
    
    
    + 1
    as generated_number

    from

    
    p as p0
     cross join 
    
    p as p1
     cross join 
    
    p as p2
     cross join 
    
    p as p3
     cross join 
    
    p as p4
     cross join 
    
    p as p5
     cross join 
    
    p as p6
     cross join 
    
    p as p7
     cross join 
    
    p as p8
     cross join 
    
    p as p9
     cross join 
    
    p as p10
     cross join 
    
    p as p11
    
    

    )

    select *
    from unioned
    where generated_number <= 3653
    order by generated_number



),

all_periods as (

    select (
        

    cast('2020-01-01' as date) + ((interval '1 day') * (row_number() over (order by generated_number) - 1))


    ) as date_day
    from rawdata

),

filtered as (

    select *
    from all_periods
    where date_day <= cast('2030-01-01' as date)

)

select * from filtered


)

select cast(date_day as date) as date_day
from days

model.owner.stg_gtm__expenses_advertisin.description: Staging model for monthly advertising spend. One row per calendar month. Parses European-formatted currency and Mon-YY date from source. Covers paid social and search (Facebook/IG, Google) for Jan–Jun 2024.

expense_advertising_sk.description:
Surrogate primary key generated via `dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key()`. Deterministic
md5 hash of the natural key column(s). Used as the stable join key for downstream models.

month_date.name: month_date
month_date.description:
Month label in `Mon-YY` format (e.g. `Jan-24`). Parsed to the first day of that month
as a `date` type in staging (e.g. `2024-01-01`).

advertising_spend_usd.name: advertising_spend_usd
advertising_spend_usd.description:
Raw value exported from internal finance system using European number formatting:
`US$` prefix, space as thousands separator, comma as decimal separator (e.g. `US$ 55 779,40`).
Parsed to numeric in staging by stripping non-numeric characters and replacing comma with period.

-- natural key: month_date
-- source: gtm_case.expenses_advertising



with source as (
    select * from "owner"."gtm_case_dev"."expenses_advertising"
),

cleaned as (

        to_date(
            '01-' || month,
            'DD-Mon-YY'

        )::numeric(12, 2)                       as advertising_spend_usd

)

model.owner.stg_gtm__leads__audit.raw_code:
-- grain: one row per audit finding
-- natural key: lead_id + audit_category
-- source: gtm_case.leads (raw, pre-cleaning)
--
-- purpose: dead letter queue for source data anomalies in leads.
--   runs alongside stg_gtm__leads as a companion view.
--   does not filter or block — surfaces findings for monitoring and downstream use.
--
-- categories:
--   millennium_date_defect    form_submission_date stored as 0024-xx-xx
--   dirty_status_value        status = 'Incorrect_Contact_Data' (underscore variant)
--   negative_speed_to_contact first_sales_call_date precedes form_submission_date
--     — likely outbound-first leads later reclassified as inbound; not removed
--   stale_lead                form_submission_date in 2020, first contact in 2024
--     — real values, not defects; flagged for recency filtering in speed analysis
--   converted_missing_opp     is_converted = true but converted_opportunity_id is null
--     — indicates a crm linkage gap; these leads show as converted with no traceable opp

{{
    config(
        materialized='view',
        contract={"enforced": true}
    )
}}

with source as (
    select * from {{ source('gtm_case', 'leads') }}
),

millennium_defect as (
    select
        lead_id                                                         as record_key,
        'millennium_date_defect'                                        as audit_category,
        'form_submission_date contains 0024-xx-xx prefix'               as audit_detail,
        'raw value: ' || coalesce(form_submission_date::varchar, 'null')
                                                                        as diagnostic_info
    from source
    where form_submission_date is not null
      and form_submission_date::varchar like '0024-%'
),

dirty_status as (
    select
        lead_id                                                         as record_key,
        'dirty_status_value'                                            as audit_category,
        'status contains underscore variant: Incorrect_Contact_Data'    as audit_detail,
        'raw value: ' || status                                         as diagnostic_info
    from source
    where status = 'Incorrect_Contact_Data'
),

negative_speed as (
    select
        lead_id                                                         as record_key,
        'negative_speed_to_contact'                                     as audit_category,
        'first_sales_call_date precedes form_submission_date'           as audit_detail,
        'speed_hours: ' || round(
            extract(epoch from (
                {{ clean_timestamp('first_sales_call_date') }}
                - {{ fix_millennium_date('form_submission_date') }}::timestamp
            )) / 3600.0,
            1
        )::varchar
                                                                        as diagnostic_info
    from source
    where {{ clean_timestamp('first_sales_call_date') }} is not null
      and {{ fix_millennium_date('form_submission_date') }} is not null
      and {{ clean_timestamp('first_sales_call_date') }}
            < {{ fix_millennium_date('form_submission_date') }}::timestamp
),

stale_leads as (
    select
        lead_id                                                         as record_key,
        'stale_lead'                                                    as audit_category,
        'form_submission_date in 2020, first contact not until 2024'    as audit_detail,
        'submission: ' || {{ fix_millennium_date('form_submission_date') }}::varchar
            || ' | first_call: ' || coalesce(
                {{ clean_timestamp('first_sales_call_date') }}::varchar,
                'null'
            )                                                           as diagnostic_info
    from source
    where {{ fix_millennium_date('form_submission_date') }} < '2021-01-01'::date
      and {{ clean_timestamp('first_sales_call_date') }} >= '2024-01-01'::timestamp
),

converted_missing_opp as (
    select
        lead_id                                                         as record_key,
        'converted_missing_opp'                                         as audit_category,
        'lead status is Converted but converted_opportunity_id is null' as audit_detail,
        'status: ' || coalesce(status, 'null')                         as diagnostic_info
    from source
    where status = 'Converted'
      and (converted_opportunity_id is null or trim(converted_opportunity_id) = '')
),

unioned as (
    select * from millennium_defect
    union all
    select * from dirty_status
    union all
    select * from negative_speed
    union all
    select * from stale_leads
    union all
    select * from converted_missing_opp
),

final as (
    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['record_key', 'audit_category']) }}
                                                                        as audit_sk,
        'leads'::varchar                                                as source_table,
        record_key::varchar,
        audit_category::varchar,
        audit_detail::varchar,
        diagnostic_info::varchar
    from unioned
)

select * from final

Cleans date defects, normalizes status values, parses European decimal GMV, and strips Python list syntax from array fields. Array fields are not exploded here — bridge tables built in intermediate layer.

status.name: status
status.description: Normalized lead lifecycle status. Dirty value 'Incorrect_Contact_Data' unified to 'Incorrect Contact Data'. Values: New, Working, Demo Set, Sales Nurture, Marketing Nurture, Converted, Disqualified, Not Interested, Incorrect Contact Data, No Longer With Company, No Show, Postpone, Restaurant Confirmed, Nurturing.

is_disqualified.name: is_disqualified
is_disqualified.description: True if lead is in a terminal non-converting state: Disqualified, Not Interested, Incorrect Contact Data, No Longer With Company.

sales_call_count.name: sales_call_count
sales_call_count.description: Total calls made to this lead. 21% null.

sales_text_count.name: sales_text_count
sales_text_count.description: Total texts sent to this lead. 52% null.

sales_email_count.name: sales_email_count
sales_email_count.description: Total emails sent to this lead. 48% null.

total_activity_count.name: total_activity_count
total_activity_count.description:
Sum of all logged sales touchpoints for a lead: calls + texts + emails.
Null counts coalesced to 0 before summing. Measures engagement intensity —
higher activity counts on unconverted leads may signal friction or poor fit,
while lower counts on converted leads may signal high-quality prospect targeting.

connected_with_decision_maker.name: connected_with_decision_maker
connected_with_decision_maker.description: Boolean flag — whether SDR reached a decision maker.

location_count.name: location_count
location_count.description: Number of restaurant locations for this lead. 4% null.

Estimated monthly Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) if the restaurant adopts Owner.
Sourced from `leads.predicted_sales_with_owner`. Parsed from European decimal format
(comma separator) in staging.

Used as an LTV proxy throughout this project — actual transaction-level revenue data
is not available in the source dataset. Owner's pricing model generates revenue via
a 5% take rate on online orders, so `predicted_monthly_gmv_usd * 0.05 * 12` yields
an estimated annual revenue contribution per customer.

model.owner.stg_gtm__leads.raw_code:
-- grain: one row per lead
-- natural key: lead_id
-- source: gtm_case.leads
--
-- data quality notes:
--   - form_submission_date only: has millennium prefix defect ('0024-...' not '2024-...')
--     corrected via fix_millennium_date macro
--   - all other date/timestamp fields are clean
--   - predicted_sales_with_owner: european decimal format — parsed via parse_european_numeric macro
--   - status: one dirty value 'Incorrect_Contact_Data' normalized to 'Incorrect Contact Data'
--   - array fields: stringified python lists cleaned via clean_category_list macro
--     full explode into bridge tables happens in intermediate layer
--   - converted_opportunity_id: populated on exactly 2,794 rows — matches opportunities grain

{{
    config(
        materialized='view',
        contract={"enforced": true}
    )
}}

with source as (
    select * from {{ source('gtm_case', 'leads') }}
),

cleaned as (
    select
        -- keys
        lead_id,
        nullif(trim(converted_opportunity_id), '')                          as converted_opportunity_id,

        -- lead status — normalize dirty duplicate value
        case
            when status = 'Incorrect_Contact_Data' then 'Incorrect Contact Data'
            else status
        end                                                                 as status,

        -- derived status flags
        case when status = 'Converted' then true else false end             as is_converted,
        case when status in (
            'Disqualified', 'Not Interested', 'Incorrect Contact Data',
            'Incorrect_Contact_Data', 'No Longer With Company'
        ) then true else false end                                          as is_disqualified,

        -- engagement activity counts
        sales_call_count::integer                                           as sales_call_count,
        sales_text_count::integer                                           as sales_text_count,
        sales_email_count::integer                                          as sales_email_count,

        -- total activity count derived for downstream scoring
        coalesce(sales_call_count::integer, 0)
            + coalesce(sales_text_count::integer, 0)
            + coalesce(sales_email_count::integer, 0)                      as total_activity_count,

        -- decision maker flag
        connected_with_decision_maker::boolean                             as connected_with_decision_maker,

        -- restaurant firmographics
        location_count::integer                                             as location_count,

        -- predicted gmv: european decimal → numeric
        {{ parse_european_numeric('predicted_sales_with_owner') }}          as predicted_monthly_gmv_usd,

        -- array fields: clean python list syntax, null empty lists
        {{ clean_category_list('marketplaces_used') }}                      as marketplaces_used_cleaned,
        {{ clean_category_list('online_ordering_used') }}                   as olo_tools_cleaned,
        {{ clean_category_list('cuisine_types') }}                          as cuisine_types_cleaned,

        -- dates: form_submission_date only has millennium prefix defect
        {{ fix_millennium_date('form_submission_date') }}                   as form_submission_date,

        -- timestamps: all clean, no defect
        {{ clean_timestamp('first_sales_call_date') }}                      as first_sales_call_at,
        {{ clean_timestamp('first_text_sent_date') }}                       as first_text_sent_at,
        {{ clean_timestamp('first_meeting_booked_date') }}                  as first_meeting_booked_at,
        {{ clean_timestamp('last_sales_call_date') }}                       as last_sales_call_at,
        {{ clean_timestamp('last_sales_activity_date') }}                   as last_sales_activity_at,
        {{ clean_timestamp('last_sales_email_date') }}                      as last_sales_email_at

    from source
),

final as (
    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['lead_id']) }}                 as lead_sk,
        lead_id,
        converted_opportunity_id,
        status,
        is_converted,
        is_disqualified,
        sales_call_count,
        sales_text_count,
        sales_email_count,
        total_activity_count,
        connected_with_decision_maker,
        location_count,
        predicted_monthly_gmv_usd,
        marketplaces_used_cleaned,
        olo_tools_cleaned,
        cuisine_types_cleaned,
        form_submission_date,
        first_sales_call_at,
        first_text_sent_at,
        first_meeting_booked_at,
        last_sales_call_at,
        last_sales_activity_at,
        last_sales_email_at
    from cleaned
)

select * from final

final as (

        sales_text_count,
        sales_email_count,
        total_activity_count,

)

model.owner.stg_gtm__opportunities__audi.name: stg_gtm__opportunities__audit
model.owner.stg_gtm__opportunities__audi.description: Dead letter queue for source data anomalies in opportunities. One row per audit finding — a single opportunity_id may appear in multiple categories. Runs as a companion view alongside stg_gtm__opportunities. Does not filter or block the main staging model.

audit_sk.name: audit_sk
audit_sk.description:
Surrogate primary key generated via `dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key()`. Deterministic
md5 hash of the natural key column(s). Used as the stable join key for downstream models. Natural key: record_key + audit_category.

source_table.name: source_table
source_table.description: Source table the finding originates from. Always 'opportunities' for this model.

audit_category.name: audit_category
audit_category.description: Category of data quality finding. duplicate_row: exact duplicate opportunity_ids in source export. millennium_date_defect: close_date or demo_set_date stored as 0024-xx-xx. missing_lost_reason: closed lost stage with null lost_reason_c. missing_attribution: how_did_you_hear_about_us_c is null.

audit_detail.name: audit_detail
audit_detail.description: Human-readable description of the specific finding.

diagnostic_info.name: diagnostic_info
diagnostic_info.description: Raw field values relevant to the finding, for triage.

model.owner.stg_gtm__opportunities__audi.raw_code:
-- grain: one row per audit finding
-- natural key: opportunity_id + audit_category
-- source: gtm_case.opportunities (raw, pre-deduplication)
--
-- purpose: dead letter queue for source data anomalies in opportunities.
--   runs alongside stg_gtm__opportunities as a companion view.
--   does not filter or block — surfaces findings for monitoring and downstream use.
--
-- categories:
--   duplicate_row          exact duplicate opportunity_ids in source export (salesforce artifact)
--   millennium_date_defect close_date or demo_set_date stored as 0024-xx-xx
--   missing_lost_reason    closed lost stage with null lost_reason_c
--   missing_attribution    how_did_you_hear_about_us_c null (76% population — directional signal only)

{{
    config(
        materialized='view',
        contract={"enforced": true}
    )
}}

with source as (
    select * from {{ source('gtm_case', 'opportunities') }}
),

deduplicated as (
    -- mirror stg_gtm__opportunities deduplication so downstream audit CTEs
    -- operate on the same grain. duplicate_rows CTE intentionally reads source.
    select distinct * from source
),

duplicate_rows as (
    select
        opportunity_id                                                  as record_key,
        'duplicate_row'                                                 as audit_category,
        'opportunity_id appears more than once in source export'        as audit_detail,
        count(*) || ' total rows for this opportunity_id'              as diagnostic_info
    from source
    group by opportunity_id
    having count(*) > 1
),

millennium_defect as (
    select
        opportunity_id                                                  as record_key,
        'millennium_date_defect'                                        as audit_category,
        'close_date or demo_set_date contains 0024-xx-xx prefix'        as audit_detail,
        'close_date: ' || coalesce(close_date::varchar, 'null')
            || ' | demo_set_date: ' || coalesce(demo_set_date::varchar, 'null')
                                                                        as diagnostic_info
    from deduplicated
    where
        (close_date    is not null and close_date::varchar    like '0024-%')
        or (demo_set_date is not null and demo_set_date::varchar like '0024-%')
),

missing_lost_reason as (
    select
        opportunity_id                                                  as record_key,
        'missing_lost_reason'                                           as audit_category,
        'closed lost opportunity with null lost_reason_c'               as audit_detail,
        'stage_name: ' || coalesce(stage_name, 'null')
            || ' | business_issue: ' || coalesce(business_issue_c, 'null')
                                                                        as diagnostic_info
    from deduplicated
    where stage_name = 'Closed Lost'
      and (lost_reason_c is null or trim(lost_reason_c) = '')
),

missing_attribution as (
    select
        opportunity_id                                                  as record_key,
        'missing_attribution'                                           as audit_category,
        'how_did_you_hear_about_us_c is null'                           as audit_detail,
        'stage_name: ' || coalesce(stage_name, 'null')                 as diagnostic_info
    from deduplicated
    where how_did_you_hear_about_us_c is null
       or trim(how_did_you_hear_about_us_c) = ''
),

unioned as (
    select * from duplicate_rows
    union all
    select * from millennium_defect
    union all
    select * from missing_lost_reason
    union all
    select * from missing_attribution
),

final as (
    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['record_key', 'audit_category']) }}
                                                                        as audit_sk,
        'opportunities'::varchar                                        as source_table,
        record_key::varchar,
        audit_category::varchar,
        audit_detail::varchar,
        diagnostic_info::varchar
    from unioned
)

select * from final

analysis.owner.outbound_funnel_stage_rat.description: Verifies the README claim that outbound lead-to-demo-set rate fell from 5.3% in January 2024 to 1.8% in June 2024, and that the drop is concentrated at the top of the funnel rather than at the AE close stage. analysis.owner.outbound_funnel_stage_rat.raw_code:
-- analysis: outbound_funnel_stage_rates
--
-- purpose: verify the claim in the README that outbound lead → demo set rate
-- fell from 5.3% in January 2024 to 1.8% in June 2024, and that the drop is
-- concentrated at the top of the funnel rather than at the AE close stage.
--
-- run with: dbt compile --select outbound_funnel_stage_rates

select
    month_date,
    channel,
    leads_created,
    demos_set,
    demos_held,
    closed_won,
    lead_to_demo_set_rate,
    demo_set_to_held_rate,
    demo_to_close_rate,
    overall_conversion_rate
from {{ ref('mart_gtm_funnel') }}
where
    channel = 'outbound'
    and month_date between '2024-01-01' and '2024-06-01'
order by month_date

analysis.owner.outbound_headcount_cost_t.name: outbound_headcount_cost_trend
analysis.owner.outbound_headcount_cost_t.description: Verifies the README claim that outbound headcount cost grew 31% from January to June 2024 ($38,076 to $50,000) while CAC increased 4x. Sources salary costs from int_expenses__by_channel_month (authoritative cost upstream of mart_cac_ltv) and joins to mart_cac_ltv for won count and CAC. Shows the cost/yield divergence — cost growing slowly while wins collapsed — as the core outbound efficiency story.

month_date.name: month_date
month_date.description: First day of the calendar month.

headcount_cost_usd.name: headcount_cost_usd
headcount_cost_usd.description: Outbound salary and commission cost for the month.

total_cost_usd.name: total_cost_usd
total_cost_usd.description: Total outbound cost (headcount only — no advertising for outbound).

new_customers_won.name: new_customers_won
new_customers_won.description: Closed won customers cohort-attributed to this outbound month.

cac_ltv_ratio.name: cac_ltv_ratio
cac_ltv_ratio.description: Outbound CAC:LTV ratio for the month.

headcount_cost_growth_pct.name: headcount_cost_growth_pct
headcount_cost_growth_pct.description: Headcount cost growth vs January 2024 baseline. January = 0%.

analysis.owner.outbound_headcount_cost_t.raw_code:
-- analysis: outbound_headcount_cost_trend
--
-- purpose: verify the README claim that outbound headcount cost grew 31%
-- from January to June 2024 ($38,076 → $50,000) while CAC increased 4x.
--
-- sources salary_and_commissions_usd directly from int_expenses__by_channel_month,
-- which is the authoritative cost source upstream of mart_cac_ltv.
-- joins to mart_cac_ltv for won count and CAC to show the cost/yield divergence
-- side by side.
--
-- run with: dbt compile --select outbound_headcount_cost_trend

with expenses as (
    select
        month_date,
        salary_and_commissions_usd                          as headcount_cost_usd,
        total_cost_usd
    from {{ ref('int_expenses__by_channel_month') }}
    where channel = 'outbound'
),

cac as (
    select
        month_date,
        new_customers_won,
        cac_usd,
        cac_ltv_ratio
    from {{ ref('mart_cac_ltv') }}
    where channel = 'outbound'
),

first_month as (
    select min(month_date) as first_month from expenses
)

select
    e.month_date,
    e.headcount_cost_usd,
    e.total_cost_usd,
    c.new_customers_won,
    c.cac_usd,
    c.cac_ltv_ratio,
    round(
        (e.headcount_cost_usd
            / first_value(e.headcount_cost_usd)
                over (order by e.month_date)
            - 1) * 100,
        1
    )                                                       as headcount_cost_growth_pct
from expenses e
join cac c
    on e.month_date = c.month_date
order by e.month_date

analysis.owner.inbound_speed_conversion_.name: inbound_speed_conversion_buckets
analysis.owner.inbound_speed_conversion_.description: Validates the speed-to-first-contact conversion cliff cited in the README. Claim: inbound leads contacted within 72 hours convert at ~4x the rate of leads contacted after 7 days. Negative speed values (26 leads where call precedes form submission) are excluded as a data quality artifact. Groups inbound leads into four speed buckets and reports lead count, conversion rate, and won rate per bucket.

leads.name: leads
leads.description: Total inbound leads in this speed bucket.

converted.name: converted
converted.description: Leads that converted to an opportunity.

conversion_rate.name: conversion_rate
conversion_rate.description: converted / leads. Primary signal for speed-to-conversion cliff.

avg_speed_hours.name: avg_speed_hours
avg_speed_hours.description: Average speed to first contact in hours for this bucket.

analysis.owner.inbound_speed_conversion_.raw_code:
-- analysis: inbound_speed_conversion_buckets
--
-- purpose: validate the speed-to-first-contact conversion cliff cited
-- in the GTM opportunities section of the README.
--
-- claim: inbound leads contacted within 72 hours convert at ~4x the rate
-- of leads contacted after 7 days.
--
-- negative speed values (26 leads where call precedes form submission)
-- are excluded — these are a data quality artifact, not meaningful signal.
-- see source data quality notes in README for detail.
--
-- run with: dbt compile --select inbound_speed_conversion_buckets

select
    case
        when speed_to_first_contact_hours <= 24  then '1_le_24h'
        when speed_to_first_contact_hours <= 72  then '2_24_to_72h'
        when speed_to_first_contact_hours <= 168 then '3_72_to_168h'
        when speed_to_first_contact_hours >  168 then '4_gt_168h'
    end                                                     as speed_bucket,
    count(*)                                                as leads,
    count(case when is_converted = true then 1 end)         as converted,
    count(case when is_won = true then 1 end)               as won,
    round(
        count(case when is_converted = true then 1 end)::numeric
        / nullif(count(*), 0),
        3
    )                                                       as conversion_rate,
    round(
        count(case when is_won = true then 1 end)::numeric
        / nullif(count(*), 0),
        3
    )                                                       as won_rate,
    round(avg(speed_to_first_contact_hours)::numeric, 1)    as avg_speed_hours
from {{ ref('int_leads__enriched') }}
where
    channel = 'inbound'
    and speed_to_first_contact_hours is not null
    and speed_to_first_contact_hours > 0
group by 1
order by 1

analysis.owner.inbound_speed_recency_bre.name: inbound_speed_recency_breakdown
analysis.owner.inbound_speed_recency_bre.description: Decomposes the >168h speed bucket to understand how much of the low conversion rate is driven by genuinely stale leads (2020 form submissions contacted in 2024) vs. leads with a week-to-month response delay. 1,319 leads have speed > 10,000h — if these dominate the >168h bucket they compress the apparent conversion rate for leads with a 1-4 week delay, which is a meaningfully different and more actionable signal.

leads.name: leads
leads.description: Total inbound leads in this recency bucket.

converted.name: converted
converted.description: Leads that converted to an opportunity.

min_hours.name: min_hours
min_hours.description: Minimum speed to first contact in this bucket.

max_hours.name: max_hours
max_hours.description: Maximum speed to first contact in this bucket.

analysis.owner.inbound_speed_recency_bre.raw_code:
-- analysis: inbound_speed_recency_breakdown
--
-- purpose: decompose the >168h speed bucket to understand how much of the
-- low conversion rate is driven by genuinely stale leads (2020 form submissions
-- contacted in 2024) vs. leads with a week-to-month response delay.
--
-- context: 1,319 leads have speed > 10,000h (form in 2020, called in 2024).
-- if these dominate the >168h bucket they compress the apparent conversion rate
-- for leads with a 1–4 week response delay, which may be a meaningfully
-- different and more actionable signal.
--
-- run with: dbt compile --select inbound_speed_recency_breakdown

select
    case
        when speed_to_first_contact_hours <= 168   then '1_contacted_le_1_week'
        when speed_to_first_contact_hours <= 720   then '2_contacted_1w_to_1mo'
        when speed_to_first_contact_hours <= 8760  then '3_contacted_1mo_to_1yr'
        when speed_to_first_contact_hours >  8760  then '4_stale_gt_1yr'
    end                                                     as recency_bucket,
    count(*)                                                as leads,
    count(case when is_converted = true then 1 end)         as converted,
    round(
        count(case when is_converted = true then 1 end)::numeric
        / nullif(count(*), 0),
        3
    )                                                       as conversion_rate,
    round(min(speed_to_first_contact_hours)::numeric, 0)    as min_hours,
    round(max(speed_to_first_contact_hours)::numeric, 0)    as max_hours
from {{ ref('int_leads__enriched') }}
where
    channel = 'inbound'
    and speed_to_first_contact_hours > 0
group by 1
order by min_hours

        when speed_to_first_contact_hours >  8760  then '4_stale_gt_1yr'
    end                                                     as recency_bucket,
    count(*)                                                as leads,
    count(case when is_converted = true then 1 end)         as converted,
    round(
        count(case when is_converted = true then 1 end)::numeric
        / nullif(count(*), 0),
        3
    )                                                       as conversion_rate,
    round(min(speed_to_first_contact_hours)::numeric, 0)    as min_hours,
    round(max(speed_to_first_contact_hours)::numeric, 0)    as max_hours
from "owner"."gtm_case_dev"."int_leads__enriched"
where
    channel = 'inbound'
    and speed_to_first_contact_hours > 0
group by 1
order by min_hours

analysis.owner.july_outbound_completenes.name: july_outbound_completeness
analysis.owner.july_outbound_completenes.description: Verifies whether July 2024 outbound data represents a genuine conversion collapse or a data cutoff artifact. Key question: is the dataset simply incomplete for July, or did outbound conversion genuinely drop to near-zero? Reports lead volume, conversion rate, and date range by channel for July 2024 entries. July covers only the first 10 days and is excluded from trend analysis in the README.

analysis.owner.july_outbound_completenes.raw_code:
-- analysis: july_outbound_completeness
--
-- purpose: verify whether July 2024 outbound data represents a genuine
-- conversion collapse or a data cutoff artifact before citing in documentation.
--
-- key question: is the dataset simply incomplete for July, or did outbound
-- conversion genuinely drop to near-zero?
--
-- run with: dbt compile --select july_outbound_completeness
-- then execute compiled SQL from target/compiled/

-- 1. volume and conversion by channel for July
select
    channel,
    count(*)                                                as leads_created,
    count(case when is_won = true then 1 end)               as closed_won,
    round(
        count(case when is_won = true then 1 end)::numeric
        / nullif(count(*), 0),
        4
    )                                                       as conversion_rate,
    min(
        case when channel = 'inbound'
            then form_submission_date
            else first_sales_call_at::date
        end
    )                                                       as earliest_entry,
    max(
        case when channel = 'inbound'
            then form_submission_date
            else first_sales_call_at::date
        end
    )                                                       as latest_entry,
    max(last_sales_activity_at::date)                       as latest_activity
from {{ ref('int_leads__enriched') }}
where
    (channel = 'inbound'
        and date_trunc('month', form_submission_date::timestamp) = '2024-07-01')
    or
    (channel = 'outbound'
        and date_trunc('month', first_sales_call_at) = '2024-07-01')
group by 1

test.owner.assert_mart_leading_indicator.name: assert_mart_leading_indicators_disqualified_tier_null
test.owner.assert_mart_leading_indicator.description: Disqualified leads are excluded from conversion scoring by design — their conversion_probability_tier must always be null. A non-null tier on a disqualified lead means the is_disqualified flag and the tier CASE expression have drifted out of sync. Returns violating rows; passes when zero rows returned.

from {{ ref('mart_leading_indicators') }}
where

test.owner.assert_mart_leading_indicator.name: assert_mart_leading_indicators_converted_not_cold
test.owner.assert_mart_leading_indicator.description: A lead that actually converted should never be scored cold. The cold tier represents ~1.6% conversion rate — if a converted lead lands there, the tier logic is not capturing the signal that caused the conversion. Systematic violations indicate tier thresholds need revisiting. Converted leads that are also disqualified (null tier) are excluded — disqualification takes precedence. Returns violating rows; passes when zero rows returned.

-- represents ~1.6% conversion rate — if a converted lead lands there,

-- this is a data quality signal, not a hard correctness failure, but
-- systematic violations indicate the tier thresholds need revisiting.
--
-- note: converted leads that are also disqualified have null tier and are
-- excluded — disqualification takes precedence.

    total_activity_count,

from {{ ref('mart_leading_indicators') }}
where
    is_converted = true
    and is_disqualified is not true

test.owner.assert_mart_leading_indicator.name: assert_mart_leading_indicators_lead_count_matches_staging
test.owner.assert_mart_leading_indicator.description: The mart is lead-grain and must contain exactly one row per lead in stg_gtm__leads. A count mismatch means the incremental watermark missed leads, a join filtered rows unexpectedly, or duplicates were introduced. Returns one row with the staging vs mart delta when counts differ; passes when zero rows returned.
test.owner.assert_mart_leading_indicator.raw_code:
-- test: assert_mart_leading_indicators_lead_count_matches_staging
--
-- the mart is lead-grain and should contain one row per lead in staging.
-- a count mismatch means either the incremental watermark missed leads,
-- a join filtered rows unexpectedly, or duplicates were introduced.
--
-- returns one row with the delta when counts differ — test passes when zero rows returned.

with staging_count as (
    select count(*) as n from {{ ref('stg_gtm__leads') }}
),

mart_count as (
    select count(*) as n from {{ ref('mart_leading_indicators') }}
)

select
    staging_count.n     as staging_leads,
    mart_count.n        as mart_leads,
    mart_count.n - staging_count.n as delta
from staging_count
cross join mart_count
where staging_count.n != mart_count.n

test.owner.assert_mart_cac_ltv_outbound_.name: assert_mart_cac_ltv_outbound_cac_not_extreme
test.owner.assert_mart_cac_ltv_outbound_.description: Flags months where outbound CAC exceeds inbound CAC by more than 10x. A 10x gap is not a hard business rule but indicates either a data anomaly (near-zero outbound wins inflating the ratio) or a channel efficiency collapse worth surfacing explicitly. Jun 2024 outbound CAC ($6,250) vs inbound (~$1,500) = ~4x — within bounds. Returns violating months; passes when zero rows returned.
test.owner.assert_mart_cac_ltv_outbound_.raw_code:
-- test: assert_mart_cac_ltv_outbound_cac_not_extreme
--
-- flags months where outbound CAC exceeds inbound CAC by more than 10x.
-- a 10x gap is not a hard business rule but indicates either a data
-- anomaly (e.g. near-zero outbound wins inflating the ratio) or a
-- channel efficiency collapse worth surfacing explicitly.
--
-- june 2024 outbound CAC ($6,250) vs inbound ($1,500) = ~4x — within bounds.
-- if the ratio ever exceeds 10x, this test will surface it for review.
--
-- returns violating months — test passes when zero rows returned.

with pivoted as (
    select
        month_date,
        max(case when channel = 'inbound'  then cac_usd end)   as inbound_cac,
        max(case when channel = 'outbound' then cac_usd end)   as outbound_cac
    from {{ ref('mart_cac_ltv') }}
    where cac_usd is not null
    group by month_date
)

select
    month_date,
    inbound_cac,
    outbound_cac,
    round(outbound_cac / nullif(inbound_cac, 0), 2)    as cac_ratio
from pivoted
where outbound_cac / nullif(inbound_cac, 0) > 10

source.owner.gtm_case.leads.name: leads
source.owner.gtm_case.leads.source_description: Raw GTM source data exported from Salesforce and internal finance systems. Currently proxied via dbt seeds for local development. Target: Snowflake database `demo_db`, schema `gtm_case` on production.
source.owner.gtm_case.leads.description: One row per inbound or outbound lead. Contains restaurant firmographic attributes (cuisine, location count, marketplace presence), SDR/AE engagement activity (call/text/email counts and timestamps), and lead lifecycle status. Array-like string fields (marketplaces_used, online_ordering_used, cuisine_types) are raw Salesforce export artifacts and will be parsed in staging. Date fields contain a known export defect (millennium prefix '20' dropped) corrected in staging.

form_submission_date.name: form_submission_date
form_submission_date.description: Date prospect submitted interest form. 58% null — outbound leads have no form submission.

sales_call_count.name: sales_call_count
sales_call_count.description: Total outbound calls made to this lead. 21% null.

sales_text_count.name: sales_text_count
sales_text_count.description: Total texts sent to this lead. 52% null.

sales_email_count.name: sales_email_count
sales_email_count.description: Total emails sent to this lead. 47% null.

first_sales_call_date.name: first_sales_call_date
first_sales_call_date.description: Timestamp of first call attempt. Key leading indicator for speed-to-contact.

first_meeting_booked_date.name: first_meeting_booked_date
first_meeting_booked_date.description: Timestamp of first meeting booked. 88% null — most leads never reach this stage.

last_sales_call_date.name: last_sales_call_date
last_sales_call_date.description: Timestamp of most recent call. Used to identify stalled leads.

last_sales_activity_date.name: last_sales_activity_date
last_sales_activity_date.description: Timestamp of any most recent sales activity across all channels.

predicted_sales_with_owner.name: predicted_sales_with_owner
predicted_sales_with_owner.description: Estimated monthly GMV if restaurant adopts Owner. Raw source is varchar with European decimal format (comma separator) — cast to numeric in staging. LTV proxy given absence of transaction-level revenue data.

marketplaces_used.name: marketplaces_used
marketplaces_used.description: Stringified Python list of third-party delivery platforms the restaurant is active on (e.g. Grubhub, DoorDash). Parsed and exploded into bridge table in intermediate layer.

online_ordering_used.name: online_ordering_used
online_ordering_used.description: Stringified Python list of first-party online ordering tools in use (e.g. Toast, Square, Olo). Competitive displacement signal.

cuisine_types.name: cuisine_types
cuisine_types.description: Stringified Python list of cuisine categories for the restaurant.

location_count.name: location_count
location_count.description: Number of locations associated with this restaurant lead.

connected_with_decision_maker.name: connected_with_decision_maker
connected_with_decision_maker.description: Boolean flag indicating whether SDR reached a decision maker.

status.name: status
status.description: Current lead lifecycle status. Known values: New, Working, Demo Set, Sales Nurture, Marketing Nurture, Converted, Disqualified, Not Interested, Incorrect Contact Data, No Longer With Company, No Show, Postpone, Restaurant Confirmed, Nurturing. Note: 'Incorrect_Contact_Data' is a dirty duplicate of 'Incorrect Contact Data' — normalized in staging.

converted_opportunity_id.name: converted_opportunity_id
converted_opportunity_id.description: Foreign key to opportunities.opportunity_id. Populated only on converted leads (89% null). Join surface between leads and opportunities funnel.

source.owner.gtm_case.opportunities.name: opportunities
source.owner.gtm_case.opportunities.source_description: Raw GTM source data exported from Salesforce and internal finance systems. Currently proxied via dbt seeds for local development. Target: Snowflake database `demo_db`, schema `gtm_case` on production.
source.owner.gtm_case.opportunities.description: One row per sales opportunity. Represents a lead that has entered the formal sales process (post-demo-set). Contains funnel stage, demo logistics, attribution source, and closed lost taxonomy. Joins to leads via leads.converted_opportunity_id.

stage_name.name: stage_name
stage_name.description: Current funnel stage. Values: Interest, Demo Set, In Progress, Verbal Commitment, On Hold, Closed Won, Closed Lost.

lost_reason_c.name: lost_reason_c
lost_reason_c.description: Reason for closure on lost opportunities. 37% null (includes open and won opps). Key values: No Demo Held, No Decision / Non-Responsive, Lack of Urgency, Price, POS Integration, Bad Fit, Lost to Competitor, Not a Decision Maker.

closed_lost_notes_c.name: closed_lost_notes_c
closed_lost_notes_c.description: Free-text AE notes on lost deals. 74% null.

business_issue_c.name: business_issue_c
business_issue_c.description: Free-text description of the prospect's stated business problem. 74% null.

how_did_you_hear_about_us_c.name: how_did_you_hear_about_us_c
how_did_you_hear_about_us_c.description: Attribution channel. 75% null but directionally useful where populated. Values: Facebook/IG, Cold call, Media Outlet, Youtube, Google, Restaurant Referral, Word of mouth, Webinar, Other.

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description: Timestamp opportunity was created in Salesforce.

demo_held.name: demo_held
demo_held.description: Boolean. Whether a demo was actually conducted.

demo_set_date.name: demo_set_date
demo_set_date.description: Date demo was scheduled. 28% null. Contains same millennium prefix defect as leads — corrected in staging.

close_date.name: close_date
close_date.description: Opportunity close date. Populated for all rows but contains millennium prefix defect throughout — corrected in staging.

last_sales_call_date_time.name: last_sales_call_date_time
last_sales_call_date_time.description: Timestamp of most recent AE call on this opportunity.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.description: Salesforce account identifier. Links opportunity to restaurant account.

month.name: month
month.description: Month label in Mon-YY format. Parsed to date in staging.

outbound_sales_team.description: Total salary and commission cost for outbound (BDR) team. Raw format e.g. 'US$ 38 075,75'.

inbound_sales_team.name: inbound_sales_team
inbound_sales_team.description: Total salary and commission cost for inbound (SDR) team. Raw format e.g. 'US$ 39 216,00'.

macro.owner.fix_millennium_date.name: fix_millennium_date
macro.owner.fix_millennium_date.description:
Corrects a known Salesforce export defect where the millennium prefix is dropped from date values, producing '0024-xx-xx' instead of '2024-xx-xx'. Applies a string overlay replacing the leading '00' with '20' before casting to date. Guards against empty strings and Postgres epoch minimum ('0001-01-01'). Used in: stg_gtm__leads (form_submission_date),
         stg_gtm__opportunities (close_date, demo_set_date).

arguments.name: col
arguments.description: The date column to correct. Must be castable to varchar.

macro.owner.hours_between.name: hours_between
macro.owner.hours_between.description: Computes the number of hours between two timestamps as a float. Returns null if either input is null. Uses epoch extraction for cross-adapter compatibility (Postgres and Snowflake both support extract(epoch from interval)). Used in: int_leads__enriched (speed_to_first_contact_hours, days_in_funnel).

macro.owner.parse_european_numeric.name: parse_european_numeric
macro.owner.parse_european_numeric.description:
Parses a numeric value formatted with European conventions — comma as decimal separator, space as thousands separator, optional 'US$' currency prefix and tab character. Strips all non-numeric characters except commas, replaces comma with period, and casts to numeric with configurable precision and scale. Returns null for null or empty input. Used in: stg_gtm__leads (predicted_sales_with_owner),
         stg_gtm__expenses_advertising (advertising),
         stg_gtm__expenses_salary_and_commissions (outbound_sales_team, inbound_sales_team).

arguments.name: col
arguments.description: The varchar column containing a European-formatted numeric value.

macro.owner.derive_channel.name: derive_channel
macro.owner.derive_channel.description: Derives the sales channel for a lead from the presence or absence of a form submission date. Inbound leads submitted an interest form via paid advertising (Facebook, Google) and have a non-null form_submission_date. Outbound leads were identified and cold-contacted by the BDR team and have no form submission. Single point of truth for channel logic — if a third channel is introduced, update here only. Used in: int_leads__enriched.

arguments.name: form_submission_date_col
arguments.description: The form_submission_date column to evaluate for nullness.

macro.owner.clean_timestamp.name: clean_timestamp
macro.owner.clean_timestamp.description: Safely casts a column to timestamp by first coercing to varchar, trimming whitespace, and returning null for empty strings. Handles cases where dbt seeds land timestamp columns with unexpected empty string values rather than nulls. Used in: stg_gtm__leads (all timestamp fields).

arguments.name: col
arguments.description: The timestamp column to clean and cast.

macro.owner.clean_category_list.name: clean_category_list
macro.owner.clean_category_list.description: Cleans stringified Python list values exported from Salesforce into a comma-separated string suitable for downstream parsing and explosion into bridge tables. Strips brackets, single quotes, and surrounding whitespace using a single regex pass. Returns null when the result is empty (i.e. source value was an empty list [''] or blank). Used in: stg_gtm__leads (marketplaces_used, online_ordering_used, cuisine_types).

arguments.name: col
arguments.description: The varchar column containing a stringified Python list e.g. "['Grubhub', 'Uber Eats', 'DoorDash']".

macro.dbt_postgres.postgres__list_relati.name: postgres__list_relations_without_caching
macro.dbt_postgres.postgres__list_relati.macro_sql:
{% macro postgres__list_relations_without_caching(schema_relation) %}
  {% call statement('list_relations_without_caching', fetch_result=True) -%}
    select
      '{{ schema_relation.database }}' as database,
      tablename as name,
      schemaname as schema,
      'table' as type
    from pg_tables
    where schemaname ilike '{{ schema_relation.schema }}'
    union all
    select
      '{{ schema_relation.database }}' as database,
      viewname as name,
      schemaname as schema,
      'view' as type
    from pg_views
    where schemaname ilike '{{ schema_relation.schema }}'
    union all
    select
      '{{ schema_relation.database }}' as database,
      matviewname as name,
      schemaname as schema,
      'materialized_view' as type
    from pg_matviews
    where schemaname ilike '{{ schema_relation.schema }}'
  {% endcall %}
  {{ return(load_result('list_relations_without_caching').table) }}
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_postgres.postgres__get_show_in.name: postgres__get_show_indexes_sql
macro.dbt_postgres.postgres__get_show_in.macro_sql:
{% macro postgres__get_show_indexes_sql(relation) %}
    select
        i.relname                                   as name,
        m.amname                                    as method,
        ix.indisunique                              as "unique",
        array_to_string(array_agg(a.attname), ',')  as column_names
    from pg_index ix
    join pg_class i
        on i.oid = ix.indexrelid
    join pg_am m
        on m.oid=i.relam
    join pg_class t
        on t.oid = ix.indrelid
    join pg_namespace n
        on n.oid = t.relnamespace
    join pg_attribute a
        on a.attrelid = t.oid
        and a.attnum = ANY(ix.indkey)
    where t.relname = '{{ relation.identifier }}'
      and n.nspname = '{{ relation.schema }}'
      and t.relkind in ('r', 'm')
    group by 1, 2, 3
    order by 1, 2, 3
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_postgres.postgres__get_relatio.name: postgres__get_relations
macro.dbt_postgres.postgres__get_relatio.macro_sql:
{% macro postgres__get_relations() -%}

  {#
      -- in pg_depend, objid is the dependent, refobjid is the referenced object
      --  > a pg_depend entry indicates that the referenced object cannot be
      --  > dropped without also dropping the dependent object.
  #}

  {%- call statement('relations', fetch_result=True) -%}
    select distinct
        dependent_namespace.nspname as dependent_schema,
        dependent_class.relname as dependent_name,
        referenced_namespace.nspname as referenced_schema,
        referenced_class.relname as referenced_name

    -- Query for views: views are entries in pg_class with an entry in pg_rewrite, but we avoid
    -- a seq scan on pg_rewrite by leveraging the fact there is an "internal" row in pg_depend for
    -- the view...
    from pg_class as dependent_class
    join pg_namespace as dependent_namespace on dependent_namespace.oid = dependent_class.relnamespace
    join pg_depend as dependent_depend on dependent_depend.refobjid = dependent_class.oid
        and dependent_depend.classid = 'pg_rewrite'::regclass
        and dependent_depend.refclassid = 'pg_class'::regclass
        and dependent_depend.deptype = 'i'

    -- ... and via pg_depend (that has a row per column, hence the need for "distinct" above, and
    -- making sure to exclude the internal row to avoid a view appearing to depend on itself)...
    join pg_depend as joining_depend on joining_depend.objid = dependent_depend.objid
        and joining_depend.classid = 'pg_rewrite'::regclass
        and joining_depend.refclassid = 'pg_class'::regclass
        and joining_depend.refobjid != dependent_depend.refobjid

    -- ... we can find the tables they query from in pg_class, but excluding system tables. Note we
    -- don't need need to exclude _dependent_ system tables, because they only query from other
    -- system tables, and so are automatically excluded by excluding _referenced_ system tables
    join pg_class as referenced_class on referenced_class.oid = joining_depend.refobjid
    join pg_namespace as referenced_namespace on referenced_namespace.oid = referenced_class.relnamespace
        and referenced_namespace.nspname != 'information_schema'
        and referenced_namespace.nspname not like 'pg\_%'

    order by
        dependent_schema, dependent_name, referenced_schema, referenced_name;

  {%- endcall -%}

  {{ return(load_result('relations').table) }}
{% endmacro %}
